proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a higher mean objective_response than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
Patients with ecog_ps==2 have a lower mean objective_response than patients with ecog_ps in {0,1}.
Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 have a higher mean objective_response than those with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0 (overall main effect).
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower mean objective_response than those with tp53_mutation=0.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower mean objective_response than those with complex_karyotype=0.
Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher mean objective_response than those with npm1_mutation=0.
Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin=1 have a higher mean objective_response than those with treatment_midostaurin=0 (main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib=1 have a higher mean objective_response than those with treatment_gilteritinib=0 (main effect).
Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response than those with flt3_itd=0.
Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response than those with secondary_aml=0.

analyses.result_summary: ORR 0.221 in idh1_mutation=1 (n=3624) vs 0.165 in idh1_mutation=0 (chi-square p=3.5e-18). Strong positive main effect.
ORR 0.112 in ecog_ps==2 (n=7437) vs 0.179 in ecog_ps<2 (chi-square p=5.2e-46). Strong negative main effect.
ORR 0.173 on treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs 0.166 off (chi-square p=0.031). Small positive main effect.
ORR 0.166 in tp53_mutation=1 vs 0.169 in tp53_mutation=0 (chi-square p=0.52). No significant main effect.
ORR 0.168 in complex_karyotype=1 vs 0.169 in complex_karyotype=0 (chi-square p=0.82). No significant main effect.
ORR 0.173 in npm1_mutation=1 vs 0.167 in npm1_mutation=0 (chi-square p=0.11). Trend toward positive but not significant on main effect.
ORR 0.169 on midostaurin vs 0.169 off (chi-square p=0.92). No main effect of midostaurin in unselected cohort.
ORR 0.163 on gilteritinib vs 0.170 off (chi-square p=0.13). No main effect.
ORR 0.172 in flt3_itd=1 vs 0.168 in flt3_itd=0 (chi-square p=0.40). No significant main effect.
ORR 0.169 in secondary_aml=1 vs 0.169 in secondary_aml=0 (chi-square p=0.91). No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_midostaurin on objective_response is more positive in flt3_itd=1 than in flt3_itd=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_gilteritinib on objective_response is more positive in flt3_itd=1 than in flt3_itd=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_ivosidenib on objective_response is more positive in idh1_mutation=1 than in idh1_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_enasidenib on objective_response is more positive in idh2_mutation=1 than in idh2_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is more positive in idh1_mutation=1 than in idh1_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is more positive in npm1_mutation=1 than in npm1_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_7plus3 on objective_response is more negative in complex_karyotype=1 than in complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction).
The effect of treatment_7plus3 on objective_response is more negative in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0.

analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin x flt3_itd interaction log-OR = -0.105 (p=0.22). ORR 0.161 in (mido+, flt3_itd+) vs 0.174 in (mido-, flt3_itd+); marker-positive subgroup did not benefit. Hypothesis refuted.
Gilteritinib x flt3_itd interaction log-OR = +0.034 (p=0.69). No subgroup benefit in flt3_itd+ patients. Hypothesis refuted.

Ivosidenib x idh1_mutation interaction log-OR = -0.364 (p=0.027). Direction is OPPOSITE expectation: in idh1+ patients, ORR is 0.174 on ivosidenib vs 0.225 off. Hypothesis refuted with significant negative interaction.
Enasidenib x idh2_mutation interaction log-OR = -0.124 (p=0.45). No positive subgroup benefit. Hypothesis refuted.
Venetoclax+aza x idh1_mutation interaction log-OR = +0.730 (p=7.7e-18). ORR jumps to 0.298 in (ven+aza+, idh1+) vs 0.171 in (ven+aza-, idh1+). Strongly supports hypothesis.
Venetoclax+aza x npm1_mutation interaction log-OR = +0.261 (p=6.7e-7). Treatment effect of +3.4 percentage points in npm1+ vs -0.4 in npm1-. Supports hypothesis.
7+3 x complex_karyotype interaction log-OR = -0.213 (p=1.6e-3). 7+3 reduces ORR by 2.3 pp in CK+ vs increases by 0.7 pp in CK-. Supports negative-interaction hypothesis.
7+3 x unfit_for_intensive interaction log-OR = -0.033 (p=0.50). No significant interaction. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a more negative effect on objective_response in tp53_mutation=1 patients than in tp53_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
The effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is more positive in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0.
The effect of treatment_gilteritinib on objective_response is more positive in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a more negative response in npm1_mutation=1 than in npm1_mutation=0 (negative interaction).
ECOG performance status (ecog_ps==2) does not modify the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: VenAza x tp53_mutation interaction log-OR = -0.087 (p=0.28). No significant interaction; hypothesis refuted.
VenAza x unfit_for_intensive interaction log-OR = +0.104 (p=0.036). Treatment effect of +1.6 pp in unfit vs +0.2 pp in fit. Supports hypothesis.
Gilteritinib x unfit_for_intensive log-OR = +0.135 (p=0.048). Borderline-significant positive interaction; supports hypothesis.
Ivosidenib x npm1_mutation log-OR = -0.225 (p=0.024). In npm1+ patients, ivosidenib lowers ORR by 2 pp; supports negative-interaction hypothesis.
VenAza x ecog_2 interaction log-OR = +0.014 (p=0.86). Treatment effect is similar across ECOG groups; null hypothesis not refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response (negative continuous main effect).
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Per-SD log-OR = -0.155 (p=7.4e-35). ORR 0.187 in lowest tertile vs 0.144 in highest. Strong negative effect; supports hypothesis.
Per-SD log-OR = -0.109 (p=1.2e-19). ORR 0.188 in lowest tertile vs 0.151 in highest. Strong negative effect; supports hypothesis.
Per-SD log-OR = +0.058 (p=1.4e-6). Higher albumin associated with higher ORR; supports hypothesis.
Per-SD log-OR = -0.064 (p=2.8e-6). Higher CRP (inflammation) associated with lower ORR; supports hypothesis.
Per-SD log-OR = -0.056 (p=1.9e-5). Hyperleukocytosis associated with lower ORR; supports hypothesis.
Per-SD log-OR = -0.011 (p=0.35). LDH not associated with ORR in this cohort; hypothesis refuted.
Per-SD log-OR = +0.004 (p=0.72). No age main effect on ORR; hypothesis refuted.
Per-SD log-OR = -0.017 (p=0.15). No significant main effect; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a more positive effect on objective_response in older patients (positive interaction with age_years).
Treatment_7plus3 has a more positive effect on objective_response in younger patients (negative interaction with age_years).
Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a more positive effect on objective_response in patients with higher blast_pct_marrow (positive interaction).
Treatment_7plus3 has a more positive effect in patients with lower weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative interaction with weight loss).
ECOG performance status modifies the effect of treatment_enasidenib on objective_response (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: VenAza x age_years interaction log-OR = -0.001 (p=0.98). No age modification; hypothesis refuted.
7+3 x age_years interaction log-OR = +0.012 (p=0.61). No age modification; hypothesis refuted.
VenAza x blast_pct interaction log-OR = +0.041 (p=0.092). Borderline trend; not significant. Hypothesis weakly supported but not statistically significant.
7+3 x weight_loss interaction log-OR = +0.043 (p=0.091). Borderline; sign is opposite the hypothesized negative direction. Hypothesis refuted.
Enasidenib x ecog_ps interaction log-OR = -0.042 (p=0.37). No significant ECOG modification; hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Objective_response differs across insurance_type categories.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have a different objective_response than those with rural_residence=0.
Patients with atrial_fibrillation=1 have a lower objective_response than those without.
Patients with hiv_positive=1 have a lower objective_response than those with hiv_positive=0.
Markers typically relevant to solid tumors (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, msi_high) have null main effects on objective_response in this AML cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Chi-square p=9.6e-3. ORR ranges from 0.147 (other) to 0.173 (white); white > hispanic > asian > black > other. Supports hypothesis (race-related disparity in ORR).
Chi-square p=0.54. ORR similar across insurance types (0.166-0.172). Hypothesis refuted.
ORR identical (0.169 vs 0.169). Hypothesis refuted.
ORR 0.156 in AF+ (n=4143) vs 0.170 in AF- (chi-square p=0.025). Borderline-significant negative association; supports hypothesis.
ORR 0.141 in HIV+ (n=488) vs 0.169 in HIV- (chi-square p=0.11). Trend toward lower ORR but not significant; hypothesis not supported.

her2_amplification p=0.86, met_exon14_skipping p=0.59, ros1_fusion p=0.77, braf_v600e p=0.53, msi_high p=0.16. None significant. Supports null hypothesis: solid-tumor markers are not associated with response in AML.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for age, ECOG, baseline labs, mutations, and treatments, ecog_ps==2 has a significant negative association with objective_response (adjusted log-OR < 0).
In the same multivariable model, idh1_mutation retains a significant positive adjusted association with objective_response.
In the same model, weight_loss_pct_6mo retains a significant negative adjusted association.
Adding the venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1_mutation interaction to the multivariable model yields a significant positive interaction term.
Adding the venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation interaction to the multivariable model yields a significant positive interaction term.
Adding the treatment_7plus3 x complex_karyotype interaction to the multivariable model yields a significant negative interaction term.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X).fit() where X includes ecog_2, age, mutations, treatments, labs
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for ecog_2 = -0.554 (p=1.1e-45). Strongly negative independent of other covariates; supports hypothesis.

analyses.code: Logit with idh1_mutation in the multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for idh1_mutation = +0.365 (p=8.2e-18). Strong positive prognostic marker net of other covariates; supports hypothesis.

analyses.code: Logit with z(weight_loss_pct_6mo) in the multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted per-SD log-OR for weight_loss_pct_6mo = -0.157 (p=1.8e-35). Strong negative independent association; supports hypothesis.

analyses.code: Logit with veno_idh1 added to multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1_mutation = +0.730 (p=6.1e-19). Strong positive subgroup-specific benefit; supports hypothesis.

analyses.code: Logit with veno_npm1 added to multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation = +0.270 (p=3.3e-7). Significant positive subgroup-specific benefit; supports hypothesis.

analyses.code: Logit with s7p3_ck added to multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for treatment_7plus3 x complex_karyotype = -0.211 (p=2.0e-3). Significant negative subgroup-specific harm; supports hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one SNP among the 24 measured SNPs has a Bonferroni-significant additive association with objective_response.
snp_rs1050828 (G6PD-associated) is associated with objective_response (nominal p<0.05) in this AML cohort.
snp_rs2032582 (ABCB1) is associated with objective_response (nominal p<0.05) in this AML cohort.

analyses.code: for snp in snps: Logit on z(snp); compare p to Bonferroni threshold 0.05/24 = 2.08e-3
analyses.result_summary: Top SNP rs1050828 has p=5.3e-3, which does NOT meet Bonferroni threshold (2.08e-3). 0/24 SNPs survive Bonferroni correction. Hypothesis refuted.
Per-SD log-OR = +0.032 (p=5.3e-3). Nominal but not multiple-comparison significant; partially supports hypothesis at unadjusted level.
Per-SD log-OR = -0.024 (p=0.049). Borderline nominal significance; not robust after correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three-way interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1_mutation x npm1_mutation is positive: ven+aza benefit is dramatically greater in idh1+/npm1+ co-mutated patients than predicted by the two-way interactions alone.
The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is similar across race_ethnicity groups (no race x treatment interaction).
Sex_female does not modify the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response.
The harmful effect of treatment_7plus3 in complex_karyotype is similar in older and younger patients.
Within idh1_mutation=1 patients, the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is large and positive (subgroup-specific stratified test).

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coefficient = +2.144 (p=3.0e-29). Stratified ORR: idh1+/npm1+/venAza+ = 0.621 (n=427) vs 0.168 in same genotype off treatment; +45 percentage-point absolute difference; in single-mutated subgroups the benefit is small. Strongly supports hypothesis: there is a co-mutation amplification of ven+aza efficacy.

analyses.code: LR test of model with vs without treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x C(race_ethnicity)
analyses.result_summary: LR=6.72, df=4, p=0.15. No significant treatment x race interaction overall; supports null hypothesis. (Stratified estimates: ven+aza benefit was similar in white/black/hispanic/asian and inverted in 'other' (n=1012); no formal disparity).
Sex x venAza interaction log-OR = +0.020 (p=0.68). Null; supports hypothesis.

analyses.code: Stratified ORR check for 7+3 effect in CK by age_ge65
analyses.result_summary: In CK+/age>=65, 7+3 ORR = 0.157 vs 0.179 (-2.2 pp); in CK+/age<65, 7+3 ORR = 0.153 vs 0.178 (-2.5 pp). Effect is similar across age strata; supports hypothesis (the harm is age-independent).
Within idh1+ (n=3624), ORR 0.298 on ven+aza vs 0.171 off; chi-square p=5.0e-19; +12.6 pp absolute. Strongly supports hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final logistic model containing main effects, the venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1 x npm1 three-way interaction, the venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1 and venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1 two-way interactions, and the 7+3 x complex_karyotype interaction, the three-way veno_idh1_npm1 term retains highly significant positive coefficient.

After accounting for the three-way interaction term, the marginal two-way veno_idh1 and veno_npm1 interactions become non-significant, indicating the joint co-mutation drives the benefit rather than either single mutation.
The 7+3 x complex_karyotype interaction remains significantly negative in the final model adjusting for other covariates.

Despite the dominant prognostic and predictive signals, the model has only modest explanatory power (McFadden pseudo-R^2 well below 0.10), implying substantial unexplained variance in objective response.

analyses.code: Final multivariable Logit with veno_idh1_npm1, veno_idh1, veno_npm1, idh1_npm1, s7p3_ck, ecog_2, mutations, treatments, z-scored labs
analyses.result_summary: veno_idh1_npm1 log-OR = +2.199 (p=3.0e-30, OR=9.01). After adjusting for it, veno_idh1 main interaction = -0.053 (p=0.64) and veno_npm1 = +0.038 (p=0.50). Both two-way interactions become non-significant once the three-way is included; supports both hypotheses h10_1 (3-way significant) and h10_2 (joint mutation drives effect).

analyses.code: Same final model, look at s7p3_ck term
analyses.result_summary: s7p3_ck log-OR = -0.207 (p=2.5e-3, OR=0.81). Remains significantly negative in the fully adjusted model; supports hypothesis.

McFadden pseudo-R^2 = 0.022; AIC = 44465. Dominant signals are statistically very strong but explain only ~2% of the deviance, consistent with response being partly stochastic and only partly captured by the measured features. Supports hypothesis.
